Event: Falls for Pete while Andy is on his lengthy boat trip
Explanation: She gains a new romantic connection but emotionally harms Andy by shifting affection during his absence.

Event: Upon realizing Andy was engaged to Angela she goes nuts and ends up chucking a cake at him
Explanation: She vents anger by throwing cake, hurting Andy and damaging her own image without real benefit.

Event: She throws away disposable cameras after using up the film roll, lamenting she will never see the pictures
Explanation: She deprives herself of all photos due to ignorance, with no effect on others.

Event: Takes over from Pam in watching over Michael
Explanation: She assumes a caretaking role that strengthens her bond with Michael and helps keep him in check.

Event: Lets Michael have his eccentricities but guides him toward the right thing for collective happiness
Explanation: She balances indulging Michael with steering him toward choices that help most people, including herself.

Event: Reuses hot dog water to make them more flavourful and makes tea by boiling gatorade
Explanation: Her unconventional cooking may mildly amuse her but largely produces unpleasant or unhealthy results for others.

Event: Just signing for a shipment of pens she takes as Serious Business
Explanation: Her diligence gives her a sense of purpose and ensures mundane but useful office logistics are handled carefully.

Event: Endures Gabe steamrolling her (e.g., forcing horror movies) due to his being her superior
Explanation: She sacrifices her comfort and autonomy, while Gabe gets some benefit but also sustains an unhealthy relationship dynamic.

Event: Actively avoids Gabe and openly states she dislikes being with him
Explanation: She improves her own well-being by pulling away, though it emotionally harms Gabe.

Event: Leaves Gabe after Pam’s encouragement
Explanation: Ending the relationship greatly benefits her but hurts Gabe emotionally.

Event: Is ecstatic when Darryl chooses her to help in his schemes
Explanation: She gains excitement and camaraderie while happily joining slightly underhanded plans that may disadvantage others.

Event: Throws herself into her receptionist job with enthusiasm and diligence
Explanation: Her love for the job boosts her own satisfaction and improves office functioning and morale.

Event: Takes an immediate dislike to Holly due to protectiveness over Michael
Explanation: She feels loyal and protective but treats Holly unfairly, harming Holly’s social standing.

Event: Treats Holly in a hostile “Jerkass to One” manner
Explanation: Her antagonism gives her little tangible benefit while making interactions worse for Holly and the office.

Event: Develops a Like-A-Daughter relationship with Michael
Explanation: The mutual parental bond gives both deep emotional support and growth.

Event: Treats Michael as a Parental Substitute and is crushed when he denies it
Explanation: Her emotional overinvestment causes her pain when the role is rejected, while not really helping anyone.

Event: Michael, realizing how much she cares, decides to treat her as the daughter he never had
Explanation: He gains emotional fulfillment and provides Erin with much-needed parental support.

Event: Refuses to put out the candy Pam and Jim brought back from their honeymoon because of rules
Explanation: She preserves her own sense of rule-following correctness while mildly frustrating everyone else.

Event: Grows increasingly concerned about taking a delivery of pens due to not knowing where to put them
Explanation: Her anxiety and confusion hurt her more than anyone, while the pens eventually benefit the office.

Event: Once Dwight takes over, she follows his strict security rules to the letter
Explanation: She gains approval and security-role satisfaction while imposing inconvenient rigidity on others.

Event: Happily blasts people with steam if they get the password wrong, even Dwight
Explanation: She derives minor fun or duty satisfaction while physically and socially harming others with needless punishment.

Event: Takes charge rationally in dealing with the lice infestation
Explanation: Her leadership protects herself and others from lice more effectively than others’ approaches.

Event: Decides to leave Andy for Pete because she seeks a good man
Explanation: Switching partners promises her a healthier relationship but emotionally harms Andy.

Event: Supports Andy and tries to cheer him up when his parents go bankrupt
Explanation: Her kindness deepens their bond and alleviates his distress.

Event: Begins to fall for Pete only after Andy leaves on a boat trip and barely communicates for three months
Explanation: She gains emotional comfort and a new attachment while Andy is left neglected and ultimately hurt.

Event: Struggled to express her affection for Andy at first
Explanation: Her inability to communicate feelings mainly frustrates her and delays mutual benefit, while Andy is somewhat positively affected by her eventual openness.

Event: Participates in an Unrequited Love Switcheroo with Andy
Explanation: The mismatched timing of affection brings mutual confusion and emotional strain with little net benefit.

Event: Has a Clingy Jealous Girl attitude toward Michael in a “Father/Daughter” way
Explanation: Her overattachment offers some affection but also emotional pressure and awkwardness for Michael.

Event: Becomes clingy and jealous toward Andy when he dates Jessica
Explanation: Her jealousy harms Andy and Jessica’s relationship and her own dignity with little real gain.

Event: Veers close to being a Psycho Ex-Girlfriend toward Andy’s girlfriend Jessica
Explanation: Her obsessive hostility damages Jessica and her own reputation without improving her situation.

Event: Actively avoids Gabe but still needs Pam’s encouragement to leave him
Explanation: Pam’s push helps Erin escape a bad relationship with minimal downside to others.

Event: Claims to a client that Andy has an important phone call so he can ignore it to look better
Explanation: He enhances his image at the cost of misleading the client.

Event: Takes Andy’s request to fake a call to mean she should invent a story about his mother dying
Explanation: Her overkill lie embarrasses and harms Andy while providing her no real benefit.

Event: Ignores Andy’s attempts to get her to stop the “mother died” lie
Explanation: Persisting in the lie increases chaos and harm to Andy without helping her.

Event: Contributes to Andy’s “pathetic” comeback by claiming he is abusing her
Explanation: Her false accusation severely damages Andy’s reputation and also risks reflecting badly on her if exposed.

Event: Takes charge of clearing the lice infestation more rationally than Dwight and Meredith
Explanation: Her sensible actions protect everyone and save Dwight and Meredith from their own harmful approaches.
